headline: M.S. student at GIST, South Korea.
hero_summary: Deep learning weather models can be eerily accurate and wrong at the same time. That gap is what I study.

keywords.label: Meteorology
keywords.tooltip: My domain. Earth's components interact like a living organism — dynamic, entangled, endlessly complex. Understanding and predicting this system never stops being fascinating. Weather forecasting is a universal service — improving it means sharing that benefit with all of humanity.

keywords.label: Mathematics
keywords.tooltip: My academic hobby. I feel most at ease when my thinking is structured like math. I've spent a lot of time on graph theory, and lately I've been drawn to universal approximation — exploring what function spaces neural networks can actually cover.

keywords.label: Trustworthy AI
keywords.tooltip: When the news calls AI 'omnipotent,' I quietly think: not quite. AI has immense potential — but we need honest diagnostics first. What does it know? Where does it fail? Getting that right is how the next generation develops better. That's what I'm trying to contribute to.

academic.title: 🎓 Academic Journey
paragraphs: Master Student studying weather forecast under the supervision of Prof. Jinho Yoon at GIST.
My majors are Computer Science and Environmental Engineering with a minor in Mathematics.

research.title: 🔬 Research Passion
paragraphs: I'm interested in understanding the physics behind deep learning weather and climate models, and in making them more trustworthy. Currently, I'm exploring how the butterfly effect shows up in these models and what it means from a spectral perspective.
